concept.core_loop: Swap adjacent gems on a 7-wide grid to make 3+ matches. The survival threat lives ON the board: blight seeds on the top row every ~3.5s and spreads to one neighbor each tick; a blighted gem can ONLY be cleared by including it in a match, so every single swap is at once a puzzle move (clear colors, build combos for score) AND a defense move (purge blight before it spreads). Each second the player decides WHICH match to make: chase a big same-color combo for points, or sacrifice the combo to purge an advancing blight cluster threatening a column. If blight reaches the bottom of any column it cracks that column's wall segment; lose all segments and it is game over. Tempo is deliberately reconciled — blight ticks at puzzle cadence (one spread per ~3.5s, not a separate real-time clock) so the two genres never feel like two clocks; difficulty ramps by shortening the tick toward a floor. A telegraphed 'surge' every ~20s (board-edge glow + countdown) spreads blight twice in one tick as a tension spike on top of the steady pressure.
mechanics: 7-wide gem grid, swap adjacent to match 3+
blight corruption: seeds on the top row on a timed tick and spreads to a neighbor each tick
fusion rule: a match that includes blighted cells purges them (one action serves both genres)
per-column wall segments at the grid bottom that crack when blight breaches that column
combo/streak scoring that rewards chained clears (reward beat + progression)
telegraphed surge every ~20s: double blight spread for one tick (forcing-function spike)
difficulty ramp: blight tick interval shortens over time toward a floor
game over when all wall segments crack, plus tap-to-restart
concept.art_direction: Bioluminescent reef on deep navy. Rounded gem tiles in 5 saturated colors (coral, teal, amber, violet, lime), each with a soft additive halo drawn behind it. Blighted gems are desaturated charcoal with a pulsing magenta-rot rim and thin crackling tendrils (Line2D) reaching toward the neighbor they will infect next, so the threat is legible on the board. Background is a slow vertical gradient (navy to near-black) with faint drifting plankton dots on a parallax layer (no dead space). The wall is a glowing cyan bar across the grid bottom, segmented per column; a breach fractures that segment with jagged Line2D cracks plus a red full-screen flash and a short screen shake. Feedback beats: bright particle pop on every match; a white cleansing flash radiating from cells when blight is purged; the score readout scale-pulses on combos; the surge telegraph pulses the board edge amber with a shrinking countdown ring.
concept.differentiation_notes: The survival threat lives ON the match-3 board (blight spreads cell-to-cell and can only be removed by matching it), so every swap is simultaneously a puzzle move and a defense move — the two genres fuse into one decision instead of a defense minigame running beside the grid (the run-004/005 coexistence failure). The hook is the constant triage tension between scoring combos and purging spreading corruption on the same tiles. Caveat: orthogonal puzzle + real-time is an intrinsically hard pairing; this concept maximizes fusability by making the threat a board state rather than an off-board entity.

systems_added: removed purge score-dominance: dropped the +purged*50*combo bonus (Main.gd:560) that made a purging match strictly best — safe AND the high-score move — which is why the promised triage was inert (dominant-strategy-test failure)

cross-move STREAK multiplier (tiered x1->x1.5->x2->x3) built by consecutive scoring resolves and WIPED by a wall breach — so aggressive combo-chasing (greed) raises a rising stake that letting blight reach the wall destroys, while purging protects it (safety). Reward and safety now genuinely oppose.
streak/multiplier HUD readout under the score
depth_pass.notes: systemic-axis deepen on foreign code (a game built before these skills) to repair a SOLVED loop, applying the upgraded deepen playbook. Diagnostic: content-ceiling test flagged one blight behavior / one counter ~60-90s in; dominant-strategy test FAILED — purging scored 5x a plain clear AND removed the threat, so 'triage' had no cost. Systemic fix = add an interacting mechanic (the streak stake) so the dominant move becomes situational: now the question each swap is 'take the big combo for the rising multiplier, or break to purge the blight creeping toward a breach that would zero it'. Surfaced frozen-logic change (surface-don't-swallow): scoring behavior changed deliberately — purge no longer adds its own score; purged cells score only as the normal clear they already are (counted in _start_clear). selftest Tests 7-9 are the new proof/regression assertions; the original 6 (match/purge/spread/surge/breach/game-over) still pass as the regression guard. No uitest.gd exists for this title (swap-driven, predates the uitest requirement); the headless boot run gates the new HUD/parse. status unchanged (playable) — deepen does not advance status.
